title: Amendments to the Human Pathogens and Toxins Regulations: Public Health Agency of Canada’s Forward Regulatory Plan 2026 to 2028 - Canada.ca
content:
## Public Health Agency of Canada's Forward Regulatory Plan 2026 to 2028: Amendments to the Human Pathogens and Toxins Regulations

## Title of regulatory initiative
Amendments to the Human Pathogens and Toxins Regulations
Human Pathogens and Toxins Act
We are proposing to amend the Human Pathogens and Toxins Regulations (HPTR) to support implementation of the modernized Human Pathogens and Toxins Act , which received Royal Assent on March 26, 2026 as part of the Budget 2025 Implementation Act, No. 1.
The proposed amendments would strengthen Canada’s biosafety and biosecurity framework, and enhance regulatory oversight of higher risk activities, so that regulatory requirements remain proportionate to risk while supporting scientific innovation and public health preparedness.
The proposed amendments will focus on certain HPTA provisions that are dependent on regulations to give effect. These include but are not limited to provisions related to: foreign ownership, control, and influence (FOCI); expanded biosecurity notification obligations related to higher risk agents; expanded security clearance requirements and introduction of security screening measures for access to higher containment level facilities and related sensitive information, including a new offence.
We will also be updating exemptions for low quantity toxins, which is an initiative highlighted in the PHAC’s Progress Report on Red Tape Reduction .
An evaluation was completed on the effectiveness and efficiency of activities related to the implementation of the Human Pathogens and Toxins Act and Regulations for the period from 2015-2016 to 2020-2021. Proposed regulatory amendments will also respond to the evaluation recommendations as applicable.
The next evaluation will take place five years after the amended regulations come into force.
This regulatory initiative aligns with the Mandate Letter core priorities, and continues to support and advance Budget 2024 and Budget 2025 commitments to modernize oversight of biosafety and biosecurity in Canada. It is also associated with the Public Health Agency of Canada's Regulatory Stock Review Plan .

## Regulatory cooperation efforts (domestic and international)
This initiative is not part of a formal regulatory cooperation work plan.
The proposed changes will impact regulated parties such as academia, government, and industry working with human pathogens and toxins.
The Public Health Agency of Canada held pre-consultations on Potential Amendments to the Human Pathogens and Toxins Regulations to gain a better understanding of what changes and improvements could be made to the HPTR, specifically in the context of higher level risk facilities. Consultations took place through an online questionnaire that was made available on the GoC's website between August 4, 2023 and September 5, 2023. A What we heard report was published with a summary of the findings.
The Public Health Agency of Canada also conducted stakeholder pre-consultations between November 20, 2023 and December 15, 2023 to determine the possible costs and benefits of the proposed regulatory amendments and overall impacts across regulated parties.
The Public Health Agency of Canada is targeting spring 2027 to publish proposed amendments to the regulations in the Canada Gazette, Part I, which will provide an opportunity for the Canadian public to comment. The timing and approach for consultations will be added to this section once known.
- What we heard: Consultation on potential amendments to the Human Pathogens and Toxins Regulations
- Evaluation of the Human Pathogens and Toxins Act and Regulations Framework 2015-16 to 2020-21
- Government of Canada biosafety and biosecurity information
- Budget 2025 Implementation Act, No. 1

## Departmental contact information
Lisa Fougère Director Policy and Regulatory Affairs Regulatory, Operations and Emergency Management Branch Public Health Agency of Canada Email: cb.engagement-mobilisation.cb@phac-aspc.gc.ca

## Date the regulatory initiative was first included in the Forward Regulatory Plan

## Stakeholder / Media sensitivities
Stakeholder/media reactions are expected to be minimal. The proposed amendments support/operationalize the changes to the HPTA, which received Royal Assent on March 26, 2026. Existing regulated parties have been made aware of the Act changes and the intention to amend the regulations accordingly.
